#  Add a Link to a Drawing 
##  Background 
Use Procore's Drawing tool to link your drawing set, then easily navigate through your drawing set from your computer, or Procore mobile application, by clicking or tapping links.
##  Things to Consider​ 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * 'Read Only' or higher permissions on the project's Drawings tool.  
_Note:_ Users with 'Read Only' permissions cannot publish their markups to the Published layer.


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Drawing** tool.
  2. Click **Open** next to the drawing you want to mark up.
  3. Click the **Markup** icon in the upper right of the page.   
  
![Markup In The Drawing Toolbar](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/690eed7f1b889b05c32e8c1fa29218f8/97849b368c8d5070d7b9f4577cc64966/markup-in-the-drawing-toolbar.jpg?fm=webp&h=40)  
  
_Note:_ You can also click **Info** in the drawing log next to the drawing you would like to add an item to, then click **Open in Viewer / Markup**.
  4. Select the **Drawing** icon in the markup toolbar.  
_Note:_ See [Mark Up a Drawing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/mark-up-a-drawing).
  5. Use this tool to highlight a specific section of the drawing using either a circle or a square.  
_**Tip!**_ To draw a circle over an area, start at the upper-left corner where the edges of the left and top sides of the circle would meet. Hold the SHIFT key on your keyboard (to create a symmetrical circle), left-click and drag to the bottom right of the circle, and release the left-click.
  6. Type the drawing number or title into the "Link a Drawing" field to find and select the related drawing sheet.  
_Note:_ Changes are automatically saved.


##  Next Steps 
  * [Publish Personal Drawing Markups](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/publish-personal-drawing-markups)

title: Add a Link to a Drawing
description: How to link related drawing sheets to each other.
